description: Key terms and concepts used across the Opendatasoft/Huwise open data platform APIs

terms.definition: A collection of records organized around a specific topic, published on an Opendatasoft-powered portal. Identified by a unique dataset_id.
A single row or item within a dataset, containing typed field values and optional geometry.
The full collection of datasets available on a given Opendatasoft portal domain.
OpenDataSoft Query Language — SQL-like filter syntax used in the 'where' parameter to filter datasets and records. Supports comparisons, boolean logic, text search, and geo functions.
A field used to aggregate and count records by distinct values, enabling drill-down navigation (similar to Elasticsearch aggregations).
A bulk download of dataset records in a specific format (CSV, JSON, Parquet, GeoJSON, Shapefile, GPX, Excel, etc.).
The hostname of an Opendatasoft-powered portal (e.g., public.opendatasoft.com). Each domain has its own catalog of datasets.
Metadata fields attached to a dataset, organized by template (default, dcat, inspire, etc.). Contains title, description, license, publisher, keywords, etc.
A file (PDF, Excel, etc.) attached to a dataset, downloadable via the API.
Query parameter to limit which fields are returned in the API response (projection).
Query parameter for sorting results by one or more fields, ascending or descending.
Query parameter controlling the maximum number of results returned per page (default 10, max 100).
Query parameter for pagination — number of results to skip before returning results.
Query parameter specifying the language for query processing and response (ISO 639-1 code).
Query parameter specifying the timezone for date/time interpretation and output (IANA timezone name).
Open Data Protocol — a REST-based standard for querying data. Opendatasoft supports OData 3.0 and 4.0 at the /api/odata endpoint.
Data Catalog Vocabulary — W3C standard for describing datasets in catalogs. Opendatasoft supports DCAT export via /catalog/exports/dcat.
The corporate name of the company formerly known as Opendatasoft, operating the platform as of 2024. The platform and APIs continue under the Opendatasoft brand.
The primary REST API for Opendatasoft portals, providing read access to catalog and record data. Current version is v2.1.
The unique identifier for a dataset within a portal, used as a path parameter in API requests.
The unique identifier for an individual record within a dataset.
Hypermedia links (HATEOAS) included in API responses for pagination and resource navigation.
Capabilities available for a dataset, such as 'analyze', 'download', 'timeserie', 'geo'.
